title: coronilla minima var. clusii
intro: Coronilla minima var. clusii, also known as the dwarf crowfoot or small crowfoot, is a perennial herbaceous plant in the pea family (Fabaceae). It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce inflammation, ease respiratory symptoms, and promote wound healing, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Coronilla minima var. clusii can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed for therapeutic purposes.
